channel.title: Nature
channel.description: Nature is the foremost international weekly scientific journal in the world and is the flagship journal for Nature Portfolio. It publishes the finest peer-reviewed research in all fields of science and technology on the basis of its originality, importance, interdisciplinary interest, timeliness, accessibility, elegance and surprising conclusions. Nature publishes landmark papers, award winning news, leading comment and expert opinion on important, topical scientific news and events that enable readers to share the latest discoveries in science and evolve the discussion amongst the global scientific community.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Long-lived remote ion-ion entanglement for scalable quantum repeaters
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 02 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10177-4

Long-lived remote ion-ion entanglement for scalable quantum repeaters
item.dc:title: Long-lived remote ion-ion entanglement for scalable quantum repeaters
item.dc:creator: Wen-Zhao Liu

item.title: China is betting on ‘optical’ computer chips — will they power AI?
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 30 January 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00274-9

Semiconductor chips that process light rather than electricity could boost processing speeds and reduce energy use.
item.dc:title: China is betting on ‘optical’ computer chips — will they power AI?
item.dc:creator: Mohana Basu

item.title: Constraints on axion dark matter by distributed intercity quantum sensors
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10034-w

Amplification and optimal noise filtering in hyperpolarized noble-gas spins of observations from distributed intercity quantum sensors monitoring for unexpected transient rotations of polarized spins set parameter range constraints in the search for axion dark matter.
item.dc:title: Constraints on axion dark matter by distributed intercity quantum sensors
item.dc:creator: Yuanhong Wang
Ying Huang
Xiang Kang
Dangui Chang
Jiaxuan Xu
Yifan Chen
Szymon Pustelny
Wenqiang Zheng
Min Jiang
Xinhua Peng
Jiangfeng Du

item.title: Prethermalization by random multipolar driving on a 78-qubit processor
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09977-x

The existence of a long-lived, prethermal regime in many-body systems with tunable heating rates, driven by structured random protocols, is observed using a 78-qubit superconducting quantum processor.
item.dc:title: Prethermalization by random multipolar driving on a 78-qubit processor
item.dc:creator: Zheng-He Liu
Yu Liu

item.title: Radiation-tolerant atomic-layer-scale RF system for spaceborne communication
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10027-9

Using a wafer-scale monolayer 2D MoS2 process instead of conventional silicon-based devices to manufacture components of spaceborne communication systems demonstrates radiation tolerance, low bit error rate and long-term stability, even under much harsher radiation environments.
item.dc:title: Radiation-tolerant atomic-layer-scale RF system for spaceborne communication
item.dc:creator: Liyuan Zhu
Yang Yang
Xiangqi Dong
Xiaojian Wu
Xiaoxu Xie
Hangyu Qiu
Xiang Liu
Hao Ying
Wenzhong Bao
Xiaolei Sun
Qiang Zhao
Shunli Ma
Peng Zhou

item.title: Accurate determination of the 3D atomic structure of amorphous materials
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09857-4

A quantitative framework for atomic electron tomography enables reliable determination of three-dimensional (3D) atomic coordinates and elemental identities in amorphous materials.
item.dc:title: Accurate determination of the 3D atomic structure of amorphous materials
item.dc:creator: Yuxuan Liao
Haozhi Sha
Colum M. O’Leary
Hanfeng Zhong
Yao Yang
Jianwei Miao

item.title: Optical control of integer and fractional Chern insulators
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09777-3

Optical control of integer and fractional Chern insulators is demonstrated by circularly polarized optical pumping in tMoTe2.
item.dc:title: Optical control of integer and fractional Chern insulators
item.dc:creator: William Holtzmann
Weijie Li
Eric Anderson
Jiaqi Cai
Heonjoon Park
Chaowei Hu
Takashi Taniguchi
Kenji Watanabe
Jiun-Haw Chu
Di Xiao
Ting Cao
Xiaodong Xu

item.title: Lasing of a cavity-based X-ray source
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10025-x

Lasing with multi-pass gain is achieved in a diamond-based X-ray cavity at the European XFEL, opening a path to next-generation X-ray science.
item.dc:title: Lasing of a cavity-based X-ray source
item.dc:creator: Patrick Rauer
Immo Bahns
Bertram Friedrich
Sara Casalbuoni

item.title: Bandwidth-tuned Mott transition and superconductivity in moiré WSe<sub>2</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10049-3

Tunable moiré WSe2 bilayers realize Hubbard-model physics, exhibiting antiferromagnetism, strange metals and superconducting domes, offering a controllable platform to study high-transition-temperature superconductivity.
item.dc:title: Bandwidth-tuned Mott transition and superconductivity in moiré WSe<sub>2</sub>
item.dc:creator: Yiyu Xia
Zhongdong Han
Jiacheng Zhu
Yichi Zhang
Patrick Knüppel
Kenji Watanabe
Takashi Taniguchi
Kin Fai Mak
Jie Shan

item.title: Frequency reproducibility of solid-state thorium-229 nuclear clocks
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09999-5

The nuclear clock transition of 229mTh in 229Th:CaF2 crystals is characterized as a function of doping concentration, temperature and time, demonstrating high reproducibility and identifying ideal operating characteristics of these crystals as nuclear clocks.
item.dc:title: Frequency reproducibility of solid-state thorium-229 nuclear clocks
item.dc:creator: Tian Ooi
Jack F. Doyle
Chuankun Zhang
Jacob S. Higgins
Jun Ye
Kjeld Beeks
Tomas Sikorsky
Thorsten Schumm

item.title: Low-power integrated optical amplification through second-harmonic resonance
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09959-z

An integrated optical parametric amplifier on thin-film lithium niobate achieves more than 17 dB gain with less than 200 mW input power.
item.dc:title: Low-power integrated optical amplification through second-harmonic resonance
item.dc:creator: Devin J. Dean
Taewon Park
Hubert S. Stokowski
Luke Qi
Sam Robison
Alexander Y. Hwang
Jason F. Herrmann
Martin M. Fejer
Amir H. Safavi-Naeini

item.title: A cavity-array microscope for parallel single-atom interfacing
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10035-9

A cavity-array microscope is realized using intra-cavity lenses to create a two-dimensional array of over 40 modes, each coupled to a single atom in free-space.
item.dc:title: A cavity-array microscope for parallel single-atom interfacing
item.dc:creator: Adam L. Shaw
Anna Soper
Danial Shadmany
Aishwarya Kumar
Lukas Palm
Da-Yeon Koh
Vassilios Kaxiras
Lavanya Taneja
Matt Jaffe
David I. Schuster
Jonathan Simon

item.title: Vacuum ultraviolet second-harmonic generation in NH<sub>4</sub>B<sub>4</sub>O<sub>6</sub>F crystal
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 28 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10007-z

The fluorooxoborate crystal NH4B4O6F shows promise as a nonlinear optical material for vacuum ultraviolet light sources through second-harmonic generation, demonstrating record output energy and efficiency resulting from optimized arrangements of fluorine-based units creating asymmetric sublattices.
item.dc:title: Vacuum ultraviolet second-harmonic generation in NH<sub>4</sub>B<sub>4</sub>O<sub>6</sub>F crystal
item.dc:creator: Fangfang Zhang
Zilong Chen
Chen Cui
Zhihua Yang
Miriding Mutailipu
Fuming Li
Xueling Hou
Xifa Long
Shilie Pan
